title: Caralluma geniculata
intro: Caralluma geniculata, also known as Caralluma, is a type of succulent plant belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to the Indian subcontinent. The plant is traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including reducing body weight, improving lipid profiles, and managing blood sugar levels, primarily due to its appetite-suppressing properties. The bioactive constituents of Caralluma geniculata include pregnane glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Caralluma is often used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine and is commonly prepared as a powder or extract, as well as in capsule or tablet form, for its purported health benefits.
taxonomy: Caralluma geniculata is a species of plant in the Asclepiadaceae family, which is now commonly referred to as the Apocynaceae. This family of plants is a group of flowering plants that are native to the tropical regions around the world. The Asclepiadaceae family is known for its unique flowers that are adapted to attract pollinators. The classification of Caralluma geniculata places it in the genus Caralluma, which is a group of plants that are known for their ability to thrive in arid environments.
